content: Best practices for off-label prescribing: (1) Review available evidence (clinical trials, systematic reviews, guidelines); (2) Assess risk-benefit ratio considering patient-specific factors; (3) Consider whether standard therapies have failed or are contraindicated; (4) Discuss off-label nature with patient, including evidence quality, potential risks, and alternatives; (5) Document rationale and informed consent discussion in medical record; (6) Monitor outcomes and adverse effects carefully; (7) Report adverse events to FDA MedWatch. Insurance coverage for off-label uses varies; some require peer-reviewed literature support or compendia listing (e.g., NCCN for oncology). Pharmacists should verify evidence and counsel patients on off-label status.
